title: Appending to Files
subtitle: Add to files with >>

sections.instruction: Let's look at a file that already has some content.
sections.prompt: Type `cat shopping.txt` to see the current shopping list.

sections.instruction: Now add an item WITHOUT erasing the existing ones. Use `>>` instead of `>`.
sections.prompt: Type `echo "Milk" >> shopping.txt` to append Milk to the list.

onSuccess.message: `>>` APPENDS — adds to the end without deleting anything.

sections.instruction: Check that the original content is still there.

onSuccess.message: Three items now. Original content still there.

sections.instruction: Add one more item to practice.

onSuccess.message: Four items. You're building a file line by line.

completionMessage: Key difference: `>` OVERWRITES (replaces everything), `>>` APPENDS (adds to the end). Use `>` when you want a fresh file. Use `>>` when you want to add to an existing file.
